justifications.clarity: Technical terms (FCFF, EDGAR, XBRL, WACC) are defined before use; section-opening sentences state main points; the transition from the 'six analytical steps' summary (Section 1) to the EDGAR extraction pipeline (Section 2) is smooth and explicit.
justifications.rigor: Formulas are provided for DCF, FCFF, FCFE, the FCFF-FCFE relationship, EBIT normalisation, Monte Carlo simulation, all three evaluation metrics, and first-order error propagation; the FCFF-to-FCFE proof is marked as a sketch; the claim that embedding-based peers outperform SIC-code-based peers lacks a named empirical citation.
justifications.completeness: All eight learning objectives are addressed in dedicated sections; worked examples include a software company FCFF/DCF calculation (Section 3), a ReAct DCF trace (Section 5), and a mid-cap SaaS case study (Section 8); all three comparable selection methods (prompt-based, embedding-based, hybrid pipeline) are explained with their respective pitfalls.
justifications.pedagogy: Eight explicit learning objectives are stated at the start; difficulty progresses from foundational definitions (Section 1) through computation (Section 3) and LLM-specific methods (Sections 4-5) to uncertainty quantification (Section 6) and pipeline integration (Section 8); Section 5 opens with 'Why LLMs Cannot Be Trusted for Arithmetic', motivating the code interpreter before introducing it.
justifications.style: Notation is consistent throughout (WACC, g, tau used without redefinition); no AI filler phrases detected; prose is direct and in active voice; the Section 2 engineering constraints paragraph is notably dense but technically necessary; markdown and LaTeX math fences are correctly formatted.
